ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Se benɛ niheyo ɔ nu munyu nɛ ɔ ɔ, e dɔ lɛ nɛ e je kɛ ho; ejakaa e ngɛ nihi babauu.
translated: Those people here who eat food with their hands say no and do not do well; but look there, it is empty.

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Nɛ benɛ a fie ni ɔmɛ ɔ, e sɛ tsu ɔ mi nɛ e pɛtɛ yiheyo ɔ nine si kɛkɛ nɛ yiheyo ɔ te si.
translated: Here among the people, it is said that they tell us we should respect their words with good speech.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: nɛ a tu fo kɛ ya kpa lejɛ ɔ kpo ɔmɛ tsuo a nɔ, nɛ a bɔni hiɔtsɛmɛ womi ngɛ sahi a nɔ kɛ e ngɔ bami ngɛ he tsuaa he nɛ a nu kaa e ngɛ ɔ.
translated: We say we take up this matter to follow the path of the community that listens to us, but we do not speak well of our home to ourselves; we do this and that to our life, and we take hold of that life and listen.

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
text: Nɛ e ke: “Enɛ ɔ he je nɛ nyumu ma si e tsɛ kɛ e nyɛ ne e maa pɛtɛ e yo he, nɛ mɛ ni enyɔ ɔ tsuo a ma plɛ he lo kake?
translated: Now listen: People come here to eat their food and make them work, take their possessions, and they will sell their things here? But what is the matter they say we will do here for?

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Jeha nɛ ɔ ke nga nɔ hu nɛ mwɔnɛ e ngɛ nɛ hwɔ nɛ a kɛ woɔ abolo tsu mi nɛ Mawu miaa lɛ kikɛ ɔ, anɛ e be nɔ́ nɛ pe enɛ pee ha nyɛ nɛ nyɛ hemi kɛ yemi pi ɔ lo?
translated: Jeha says that we should not fight, for they do as they please, and we should take peace from my Mother Mawu and pray—yet do they not know that what those people say causes me this pain they inflict?

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Jeha nɛ ɔ benɛ Herode na kaa ni leli ɔmɛ sisi lɛ ɔ, e muklii mi fu wawɛɛ, nɛ e tsɔ a ya gbe jokuɛ nyumuwi tsuo nɛ ngɛ Betlehem kɛ lejɛ ɔ ni ɔmɛ a mi tsuo, ninɛmɛ nɛ ye jeha enyɔ kɛ ninɛmɛ nɛ yi ja ngɛ be nɛ e bi ni leli ɔmɛ fitsofitso ɔ nya.
translated: And Jesus was called by Herod, and when the people were talking about him, his reports were multiplying and spreading, because they said he was the child of the ruler from the town of Bethlehem, who was born among the people about whom they had prophesied. And there was great fear among them, and this fear spread through the people because they were speaking lies during that time.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Se i ngɛ nyɛ dee ke: Salmo kɛ e nyami ɔ tsuo tete mia we kaa enɛ ɔmɛ a kpɛti kake.
translated: Hear what they say has come: Salt is the water that flows abundantly upon us and makes people speak with their mouths.

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Se Yesu he nɔ nɛ e de mɛ ke: “Yi nɔ bi yayahi kɛ gba puɛli ngɛ okadi hlae; se a be mɛ okadi ko hae, kaa ja gbalɔ Yona okadi ɔ.
translated: Then Jesus said to me: This is like a sheep taking a flock among wolves; if they do not send me wolves, then I will not fight against my wolves.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Niinɛ, i ngɛ nyɛ dee ke, e maa ngɔ lɛ ngɔ to e nihi tsuo kulaa nya.
translated: Well, you say there is this here, they come walking the path that they know how to eat the food well.

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Se kɛ je piani ngmlɛ nyɔngma kɛ enyɔ kɛ ya su ngmlɛ etɛ ɔ, diblii wo ngɛ zugba a tsuo nɔ.
translated: You say play piano with the united people, say dance and sing to the people's music, but your ears are deaf.

ref: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
text: Osɔfo nikɔtɔma amɛ kɛ nikɔtɔma amɛ kɛ gua a tsuo hla lakpa odase ngɛ Yesu he, konɛ a gbe lɛ;
translated: Teacher, you will hear us say you will hear us say come teach this way of the truth of Jesus, oh, but do not take this;

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: He tsuaa he nɛ ni ɔmɛ he we nyɛ nɛ a bu we nyɛ tue ngɛ ɔ, ke nyɛ ngɛ lejɛ ɔ jee ɔ, nyɛ blɛ nyɛ nane si zu ngɔ pue si kaa okadi ngɔ ha mɛ.”
translated: He said that the people who have come have two hands, and they have hands to take and give; they have houses and children by the river path and by the working path for us.

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: Kɛkɛ nɛ e de nyumu ɔ ke: “Kpa o nine ɔ mi!” Nɛ e kpa mi, nɛ e kɛ kake ɔ sɔ.
translated: Keke said she is going to the market: “Don’t touch me!” She beats me, and she always scolds me.

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Nɛ Yesu mi mi sa lɛ ngɛ a he, nɛ e ta a yɛngmɛ omɛ a he, nɛ amlɔ ɔ a hyɛ nɔ, nɛ a nyɛɛ e se.
translated: And Jesus, my Lord, come and save me, for he does good deeds for us, for our prayers are truly answered, and we give thanks to him.

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Mlaa tsɔɔli komɛ nɛ je Yerusalem ba a ngɛ dee ke: “Beelzebul ngɛ e mi.” Ni kpa komɛ hu ke: “Mumi yayamihi a tsɛ ɔ he wami nya e kɛ hiaa mɛ ɔ nɛ.”
translated: The prophets who come to Jerusalem say to me: “They call me Beelzebul.” But my mother says: “Mother, you bring water for me to drink and also give me what I need.”

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Jeha nɛ ɔ benɛ Herode na kaa ni leli ɔmɛ sisi lɛ ɔ, e muklii mi fu wawɛɛ, nɛ e tsɔ a ya gbe jokuɛ nyumuwi tsuo nɛ ngɛ Betlehem kɛ lejɛ ɔ ni ɔmɛ a mi tsuo, ninɛmɛ nɛ ye jeha enyɔ kɛ ninɛmɛ nɛ yi ja ngɛ be nɛ e bi ni leli ɔmɛ fitsofitso ɔ nya.
translated: Mary bore Jesus, and when the virgin gave birth to the child, Herod heard about it; so he sent them to search for the young child who had been born in the town of Bethlehem, in order to find where the child was born, so that the wise men who had come to Jesus would not find the child, because Herod had intended to kill the child.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Se i ngɛ nyɛ dee ke: Salmo kɛ e nyami ɔ tsuo tete mia we kaa enɛ ɔmɛ a kpɛti kake.
translated: As the saying goes: Salmo is the fish in the small net in the water, but the hungry people are not satisfied.

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Se Yesu he nɔ nɛ e de mɛ ke: “Yi nɔ bi yayahi kɛ gba puɛli ngɛ okadi hlae; se a be mɛ okadi ko hae, kaa ja gbalɔ Yona okadi ɔ.
translated: Then Jesus came and said to me: “This is like a farmer who plants seed in the field; if they hear the word but do not obey, they will reap what they sowed.”

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Niinɛ, i ngɛ nyɛ dee ke, e maa ngɔ lɛ ngɔ to e nihi tsuo kulaa nya.
translated: Well, I told you to come, he will eat the food that has no water in the pot here.

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Se kɛ je piani ngmlɛ nyɔngma kɛ enyɔ kɛ ya su ngmlɛ etɛ ɔ, diblii wo ngɛ zugba a tsuo nɔ.
translated: If you come with the piano today, you work to do for your people's house, but we sing songs that no one listens to.
